name: LinkedIn
description: Drive LinkedIn (profile + shared posts) for the authenticated user from any AI agent. Limited scope: most LinkedIn features require Marketing Developer Platform approval. 5 tools, OAuth2 Bearer auth.
instructions:
This connector uses the LinkedIn Marketing/Profile API v2 (learn.microsoft.com/en-us/linkedin/).

**HEAVY GATING**: LinkedIn's API is one of the most restricted public APIs. Most useful endpoints (read posts, search profiles, fetch connections, manage company pages) require **Marketing Developer Platform approval** from LinkedIn — a partnership program with significant requirements (existing app + revenue commitments).

What WORKS without partnership:
  - 'Sign in with LinkedIn using OpenID Connect' product scopes: `openid`, `profile`, `email`, `w_member_social` (post on behalf of user).

**Setup**:
1. Register an app at https://www.linkedin.com/developers/apps.
2. Add the **Sign In with LinkedIn using OpenID Connect** product.
3. (Optional) Add **Share on LinkedIn** product for posting.
4. Run OAuth2 authorization-code flow with scopes `openid profile email w_member_social`.
5. Set `LINKEDIN_ACCESS_TOKEN`.

**Authentication**: `Authorization: Bearer ${LINKEDIN_ACCESS_TOKEN}`.

**LinkedIn-Version header**: required on most v2 endpoints: `LinkedIn-Version: 202405` (use the YYYYMM of the API version you're targeting). Without it many endpoints return 426 Upgrade Required.

**Person URN**: shares are posted as `urn:li:person:{your_person_id}`. Get your person_id from the userinfo endpoint.

**Out of scope here** (require Marketing Developer Platform): read other users' posts, search profiles, manage company pages, ad campaigns, analytics, posts inbox.

tools.name: linkedin_userinfo
tools.description: Get the authenticated user's profile (OpenID Connect userinfo endpoint). Returns sub (person ID), name, given_name, family_name, picture, locale, email, email_verified.

tools.name: linkedin_create_post
tools.description: Share a text or article post on behalf of the user. Required: author (URN) + commentary (text). LinkedIn requires `LinkedIn-Version` and `X-Restli-Protocol-Version: 2.0.0` headers (pinned per-tool).

author.description: URN of the author, typically 'urn:li:person:YOUR_PERSON_ID' (get from userinfo's sub field).

content.description: Optional content: {article:{source:'URL'}} for link post OR {media:{id:'urn:li:image:...'}} for image post (image upload requires a separate registerUpload call out of scope here).

tools.name: linkedin_get_post
tools.description: Get a previously-created post by URN (you can only fetch posts you authored unless you have higher-tier scopes).

tools.name: linkedin_register_image_upload
tools.description: Step 1 of image upload: register an upload. Returns an uploadUrl + image URN. You then PUT the binary to the uploadUrl out-of-band, then use the image URN in linkedin_create_post's content.media.
